title: Create the Gapminder dataset
text:
# Import necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
from gapminder import gapminder

# Rename 'lifeExp' to 'life_expectancy' and 'gdpPercap' to 'gdp_per_capita'
gapminder = gapminder.rename(columns={'lifeExp': 'life_expectancy',
                                      'pop': 'population_millions', 
                                      'gdpPercap': 'gdp_per_capita'})

# Convert population to millions
gapminder['population_millions'] = gapminder['population_millions'] / 1_000_000

# Create a new pandas DataFrame from the modified gapminder data
gapminder_df = pd.DataFrame(gapminder)

# Save the DataFrame as a CSV file
gapminder_df.to_csv('gapminder.csv', index=False)

# Print the first 3 rows of the DataFrame
print(gapminder_df.head(3))

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
This page explores how life expectancy relates to GDP per capita across countries over time

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["gdp_per_capita"], df["life_expectancy"], alpha=0.3)
plt.xscale("log")
plt.xlabel("GDP per capita (log scale)")
plt.ylabel("Life expectancy")
plt.title("Life expectancy vs GDP per capita (all country-years)")
plt.show()

title: Interactive Map
text:
This interactive map shows life expectancy by country (2007)

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

df_2007 = df[df["year"] == 2007]

fig = px.choropleth(
    df_2007,
    locations="country",
    locationmode="country names",
    color="life_expectancy",
    hover_name="country",
    color_continuous_scale="Viridis",
    title="Life Expectancy by Country (2007)"
)

fig.show()

The library used by the *country names* `locationmode` option is changing in an upcoming version. Country names in existing plots may not work in the new version. To ensure consistent behavior, consider setting `locationmode` to *ISO-3*.

title: Country Analysis
text:
This page tracks life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population over time for one country

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

country = "Canada"
c = df[df["country"] == country].sort_values("year")

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Life expectancy")
plt.title(f"{country}: Life expectancy over time")
plt.show()

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["gdp_per_capita"])
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("GDP per capita")
plt.title(f"{country}: GDP per capita over time")
plt.show()

plt.figure()
plt.plot(c["year"], c["population_millions"])
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Population (millions)")
plt.title(f"{country}: Population over time")
plt.show()

title: Gapminder: Life Expectancy and Development
text: Gapminder is a country-year dataset (1952 to 2007, 5-year intervals) with life expectancy, population, and GDP per capita for 142 countries

title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
This page explores how life expectancy relates to population size across country-years

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["population_millions"], df["life_expectancy"], alpha=0.3)
plt.xlabel("Population (millions)")
plt.ylabel("Life expectancy")
plt.title("Life expectancy vs population (all country-years)")
plt.show()
